def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    momentum_window = params['momentum_window']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window).average_true_range()
    df['momentum'] = df['close'] - df['close'].shift(momentum_window)
    
    df['long_signal'] = np.where((df['rsi'] < 30) & (df['momentum'] > atr_multiplier * df['atr']), 1, 0)
    df['short_signal'] = np.where((df['rsi'] > 70) & (df['momentum'] < -atr_multiplier * df['atr']), -1, 0)
    
    signals = df['long_signal'] + df['short_signal']
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/JPY exhibits momentum-driven behavior where extreme RSI levels combined with momentum exceeding ATR-based thresholds indicate potential continuation of trend.
